pi_items.source: Task.json metadata + Drupal taxonomy naming conventions + export_result.sh validation logic (checks for config 'taxonomy.vocabulary.brands')

Task.json metadata + Drupal field naming conventions (field_ prefix) + export_result.sh validation logic (checks for field.storage.commerce_product.field_brand and field.field.commerce_product.default.field_brand)
Web search confirmed Sony is a major electronics manufacturer. Sony WH-1000XM5 is a real product with MSRP $399-400, included in seed_products.php at $348.00 (promotional price)
Web search confirmed Logitech is a real computer peripherals manufacturer. Logitech MX Master 3S is a real product with MSRP $99, included in seed_products.php at $99.99
Web search confirmed Google (Alphabet Inc.) is a real technology company/brand. Not included in current seed data but valid brand term
seed_products.php line 45: exact product title in database seed script. Web search confirmed this is a real Sony product with accurate specifications
seed_products.php line 49: exact product title in database seed script. Web search confirmed this is a real Logitech product with accurate specifications
seed_products.php line 45. Web search shows Sony WH-1000XM5 MSRP is $399-400, but promotional pricing $298-348 is common
seed_products.php line 49. Web search confirms Logitech MX Master 3S MSRP is approximately $99

privileged_info_summary: This task implements a Drupal Commerce brand taxonomy system. All verified facts: (1) The vocabulary must be named 'brands' (standard Drupal taxonomy naming convention), (2) The field must be named 'field_brand' (standard Drupal entity reference field convention), (3) The taxonomy should contain three real brand terms: Sony, Logitech, and Google, (4) The system must tag two real products with their brands: Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Headphones (real product, MSRP ~$399) with 'Sony' brand, and Logitech MX Master 3S Wireless Mouse (real product, MSRP ~$99) with 'Logitech' brand. Data is synthetically created using seed scripts in a Drupal Commerce environment, but all product and brand references are to real, existing commercial products and manufacturers.
